114324.explanation: It is important that knowledgeable and motivated administrators take seats in the newly formed municipal executive board. A reasonable balance between men and women, just like diversity in age, is preferred.

114325.explanation: The members of the municipal council are elected by the people as representatives to make decisions conscientiously. These elected politicians have received the mandate from voters during elections.

114331.explanation: The municipality of Gennep already contributes significantly by sheltering approximately 425 Ukrainian refugees who fled from war violence.

114334.explanation: Provided wood stoves are used as intended (burning clean, dry wood), these may continue to be used in accordance with regulations.

114335.explanation: It is not the municipality's task to ban terrace heaters. If necessary or desired, this should be regulated centrally by the national government.

114336.explanation: If there is a need for a centrally organized fireworks show, the municipality should facilitate this.

114343.explanation: It is important that every child can take accessible music lessons if desired. Subsidy schemes are available for children from low-income families who want to take music lessons.

114346.explanation: It is important that enforcement officers (BOA’s) are more visible in the streets, neighborhoods, and districts. This is currently insufficient. Visible BOA’s increase the feeling of safety.

114347.explanation: Many shops in the center already have CCTV for the protection of their own property. To increase street safety, more visible supervision by police and BOA’s is desired.

114348.explanation: In Gennep there is already a residential complex where people in urgent need of shelter can receive temporary accommodation.

114352.explanation: Permanent residence in holiday homes detracts from the tourist offer of the municipality of Gennep. If holiday homes are no longer interesting from a tourism perspective, it can be considered to convert them into residential housing.

114353.explanation: To enable the growth of our municipality (in terms of residents), housing construction outside the village boundaries is necessary. In many cases, expanding village centers is also easier and faster to realize by adding homes through infill locations.

114355.explanation: Supporting people in financial distress is important. There are numerous support schemes for people with low incomes. In extreme cases, municipal tax forgiveness can also be applied.

114356.explanation: The municipal threshold for supporting people at social minimum income level is set at 120%. We want to maintain this threshold.

114357.explanation: It is important to detect and prevent problems among youth at an early stage. That is why the work and deployment of youth workers are of great importance.

114358.explanation: It is not the municipality's task to designate more charging points for electric cars. If the market demands more charging points for electric cars, it is desirable that the municipality provides space for this. This is to encourage the demand for more sustainable transportation.

114359.explanation: Reducing the speed to 30 km/h may be desirable for safety reasons on certain roads, but not generally due to traffic flow considerations.

114360.explanation: The municipality primarily determines which parties provide care. Regarding care provision, alignment with Dutch norms and values is desired.

114361.explanation: Youth who need care must receive it. Quality of the care provided must be paramount. Of course, we must also keep costs under control.

114362.explanation: The property tax (OZB) may not increase in the coming years, except by the inflation rate.

114363.explanation: The dog tax should not be abolished, but it should not be higher than strictly necessary to cover the costs of the measures taken. So, only cost-covering and not a profit model.

114367.explanation: The municipality must work harder in agreements with housing corporations to allocate homes to people from our own municipality. The municipality is, however, obliged to house status holders.

114368.explanation: It is important that waste drop-off is easily accessible. Delivery of reusable waste streams, such as paper, green waste, glass, electrical equipment, and metal, should be free of charge. Thus, payment would only be required for delivery of (unprocessable) residual waste.

114369.explanation: The installation of solar panels contributes to the degradation of the landscape quality and should therefore not be allowed. There are still enough roofs on homes and business buildings available to install solar panels for sustainability.

114372.explanation: The municipality of Gennep must implement a strict anti-drugs policy. Coffeeshops contribute to a reduced sense of safety among people.

114374.explanation: The construction of a second Pageflat can give a substantial boost to the availability of affordable housing for young and old.

114375.explanation: If renting out rooms separately in a home contributes to a need, for example, for young people to live independently, then it is fine to provide for this. It will contribute only limitedly to reducing the housing shortage.

114377.explanation: As more homes will be built in the center in the coming years, more parking spaces are also needed in the center.

114379.explanation: The principle is that sports must remain possible in every village. Associations will be facilitated to cooperate. Possibly, this could lead to the consolidation of activities in the long term. Sports parks must remain sufficiently attractive.

114380.explanation: Gennep has positively put itself on the map in recent years with the Ladies Tour. This has resulted in national recognition. This can easily be built upon with an event that is well organized.

114324.explanation: We want a diverse and balanced executive board, with attention to men, women, and other differences. You compose an executive board based on quality and cooperation. This does not fit in a fixed calculation.

114325.explanation: Residents must have influence on major decisions. A referendum can help with this. However, it must be clear in advance what influence the referendum has and what will happen with the outcome.

114331.explanation: Gennep already accommodates many people, such as Ukrainians. If extra accommodation is needed, for example due to the distribution law, we will take our responsibility.

114334.explanation: Wood burning affects health and causes nuisance. At the same time, many people consider it important for their living enjoyment. We opt for clear rules, less nuisance, and clean techniques, such as filters, possibly with subsidies.

114335.explanation: Terrace heating is primarily a choice of entrepreneurs. The municipality can help stimulate sustainable alternatives. This way, we guide towards the future without a strict ban.

114336.explanation: With a national fireworks ban, New Year's Eve calls for new choices. We find it important to involve residents in this. The municipality can support initiatives without preemptively formalizing them.

114343.explanation: Music is important for children's development. The municipality does not have to pay for music lessons for every child, but we do find it important that children are exposed to music at primary school. Support is available for music lessons for children from low-income families.

114346.explanation: Safety is more than just enforcement. It also involves prevention, visibility, and social cohesion in neighborhoods and villages. The right mix is more important than simply having more enforcement officers (BOA's).

114347.explanation: Camera surveillance can sometimes help with safety. But it is not a standard solution. Deployment must always be targeted, temporary, and respectful of privacy.

114348.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be accommodated quickly and safely. For a municipality like Gennep, having its own shelter is not feasible. Good, easily accessible regional shelters offer more safety and work better.

114352.explanation: We recognize that permanent residence in holiday homes can be a solution to housing shortages for some. For our municipality, we see few appropriate possibilities for this, and over time it leads to problems with livability and facilities. Housing shortage mainly demands structural solutions.

114353.explanation: The housing task in Gennep is significant. Opportunities to build exist on the edges of the centers. We will do this carefully, with attention to greenery and livability. We want no fragmented building but coherent residential neighborhoods, so affordable housing becomes better possible.

114355.explanation: Tax forgiveness helps people with low income to stay financially afloat. It prevents debts and bigger problems. Ending forgiveness makes the situation for this group more difficult.

114356.explanation: The costs of housing, energy, and groceries have risen sharply. As a result, even people with somewhat higher incomes struggle to make ends meet. By raising the income threshold, aid better aligns with reality.

114357.explanation: Research shows that youth work helps young people connect with others and with society. It can signal and prevent problems earlier. This makes investing in youth work wise, also in the long term.

114358.explanation: Charging points should logically grow with the use of electric cars. We also want to make it easier to charge from one's own home, including for people with terraced houses.

114359.explanation: 30 km/h makes streets safer and more pleasant to live in. That is why 30 is the standard within built-up areas and 50 the exception, for example on main roads where it is safe and logical.

114360.explanation: It is important that care aligns with a person's faith, culture, or beliefs. Care purchased by the municipality can pay attention to this, but due to availability, quality, and affordability, full choice is not always possible. Much care is also arranged nationally.

114361.explanation: Quality must come first in youth assistance. The cheapest provider is not always the best for the child. Costs are important but must be balanced with quality, continuity, and appropriate support.

114362.explanation: Lowering the property tax (OZB) sounds appealing but has direct consequences. The OZB in Gennep is already below average. Lowering it means less money for amenities and livability. We choose honesty and maintaining quality.

114363.explanation: We took the initiative to abolish the dog tax. The municipal council supported this proposal, but the abolition has not yet been implemented. The dog tax is unfair and must be abolished.

114367.explanation: The housing of refugees with a residence permit follows national agreements. The municipality must comply with this, with an emphasis on a fair distribution across centers and locations. This maintains a balance with other home seekers, such as starters.

114368.explanation: Submitting waste does not have to be free. Costs should not be a barrier and must encourage proper separation. Therefore, some waste streams must be delivered free of charge and easily accessible, such as used cooking oil, because improper disposal causes great damage.

114369.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land can contribute to the energy transition. This requires tailor-made solutions per location. First, we use logical alternatives, such as roofs and existing sites. If that is not sufficient, we are open to plans that fit the landscape and surroundings.

114372.explanation: There are regional agreements about coffeeshops that work well. Therefore, this is not a simple yes/no issue. If anything changes, it must fit with safety, the environment, and enforceability. This requires careful consideration with the region.

114374.explanation: By high residential flats we mean buildings with more than ten storeys. This does not suit the scale, character, and liveability of Gennep. We choose housing construction that matches the existing environment.

114375.explanation: Liveability in neighborhoods remains guiding. Gennep is not a student city and large-scale room rental does not fit here. We focus on shared housing where appropriate, but prevent that homes are structurally divided for room rental, for example for temporary labor workers.

114377.explanation: More parking spaces in the center make the area less attractive. We prefer parking on the edges of the center, more space for bicycles, and preserving unique places like the Groene Gracht.

114379.explanation: Not all sports parks can automatically remain open. The municipality must make choices together with sports clubs. Liveability is important: sports parks can play a continuing role in the villages in an adjusted or scaled-down form.

114380.explanation: The Ladies Tour brings sport and visibility to Gennep. Having start and finish here can be valuable, as long as residents and entrepreneurs benefit and the impact on accessibility and liveability is well considered.

114324.explanation: The Gennep VVD has provided the only woman in the executive board for 8 years. Not because a woman must be there per se, but for reasons of quality. We see little value in quotas.

114325.explanation: The municipal council is elected to make considerations. A referendum reduces complex dossiers to a simple yes/no and can give a distorted picture if turnout is low. Better: good participation, online consultations, and residents' evenings.

114331.explanation: The VVD opposes the establishment of an asylum seekers center in the municipality of Gennep.

114334.explanation: During the energy crisis, many residents invested in a wood/pallet stove and want to heat their homes more independently from gas. We support this development. Therefore, we see no point in banning wood burning in our municipality.

114335.explanation: The VVD wants more space for entrepreneurs to do business and less regulation.

114336.explanation: The VVD believes such initiatives should come from the grassroots. It would be nice if, after a wood burning ban, each village had a fireworks show, but we leave this to local organizers. We gladly facilitate these organizers regarding the necessary permits, etc.

114343.explanation: Music lessons should be accessible, but a municipal subsidy for all children is not necessary. Focus support on those who truly cannot afford it and encourage offerings via associations and cultural partners. That is fairer and more affordable.

114346.explanation: The VVD supports more police presence on the streets. When that is not possible via the police, investing in our BOAs (officials with enforcement powers) is necessary.

114347.explanation: We are not in favor of a city center full of cameras, but camera surveillance may be necessary at crime hotspots.

114348.explanation: A facility like a women's shelter is better organized regionally than by a municipality the size of Gennep. Regional cooperation is the starting point.

114352.explanation: In this housing crisis, vacation homes sometimes offer a solution for home seekers. Enforcing against this and evicting people is not appropriate in this time.

114353.explanation: The VVD is the housing construction party of the municipality of Gennep. If we want to build suitable homes for all home seekers, we cannot avoid doing this outside existing village boundaries.

114355.explanation: We want to prevent poverty, but debt forgiveness is not always the most effective support. Prefer targeted help: income support, debt assistance, and tailored solutions. This way we help those in need and keep municipal finances fair and sustainable.

114356.explanation: We want to help people in trouble well, but keep arrangements targeted and affordable. A higher income limit means less room for those who really need it. Better: tailored solutions, debt help, and support towards work.

114357.explanation: We question whether the current form of youth work is the best. The VVD believes this can be done more efficiently and better.

114358.explanation: It is a given that more and more electric cars are driving around. Gennep is not an urban municipality; residents with an electric car often have their own driveway. The number of public charging points should not be a goal in itself, but we should look at how to best meet the demand.

114359.explanation: Traffic safety is very important; 30 km/h within the core is a good starting point, but not for the major exit roads. We therefore disagree that all roads in the built-up area should be 30 km/h.

114360.explanation: Everyone deserves appropriate care. We encourage choice and attention to faith/culture, but cannot guarantee this absolutely. More important: sufficient supply, quality, and short waiting times, so people can truly choose.

114361.explanation: We want to provide the best care and support to everyone. Quality comes first, but to be honest, cost control is important to keep care affordable and able to be provided in the future. We cannot avoid keeping providers sharp on pricing.

114362.explanation: The VVD is a great advocate of a smaller government and lower taxes!

114363.explanation: The VVD considers the dog tax an outdated form of taxation and wants to abolish it.

114367.explanation: As Gennep VVD, we find it only logical that someone registered for 15 years is given priority over a status holder without registration time.

114368.explanation: The VVD supports investigating a system in which waste costs are substantially reduced. The environmental site can also be included.

114369.explanation: The VVD finds it inappropriate to install solar parks in our beautiful landscape.

114372.explanation: We as VVD oppose this. Often, the arrival of a coffeeshop is accompanied by nuisance and drug tourism, which we find undesirable.

114374.explanation: The VVD sees high-rise buildings as a solution in certain places, for example overlooking the Maas, as recently built in Cuijk.

114375.explanation: We oppose this; this is exactly how room rental arises among labor migrants. The VVD wants stricter rules and more control here, because unfortunately there are many examples of nuisance with this issue.

114377.explanation: Due to the addition of hundreds of homes in and around the center of Gennep, more parking spaces are also needed. We cannot avoid this.

114379.explanation: In the long term, sports clubs will collaborate more and the number of sports parks will decrease. The VVD considers it important that something is returned for this and that sports facilities remain in every village. For example, more sports and play streets (like in Ottersum) could be considered.

114380.explanation: Agreed, but not at any cost. Being the start and finish location of the Ladies Tour costs the municipality a lot of money. We find this undesirable. We want to have a conversation about this with the organizers of the Ladies Tour.

114324.explanation: Equal representation of men and women in the executive board is an important and valuable goal, but not an absolute requirement. By allowing room for quality, support, and context, both equality and good governance are properly addressed.

114325.explanation: Residents of Gennep should be able to participate in discussions about important topics through various forms of participation. A referendum can be an additional instrument in this, but it is not the only or always the best form. Good participation requires customization, where dialogue, input, and involvement are central.

114331.explanation: But if we must take responsibility according to the distribution law, we impose restrictions on the COA: small scale, focused on families, and fitting within our community.

114334.explanation: Provided that the right wood is used.

114336.explanation: We are positive when initiatives arise from the community.

114343.explanation: Music associations are already subsidized partly for this purpose.

114348.explanation: There is a collaboration with the Nijmegen location/region.

114353.explanation: The need for housing is still large.

114356.explanation: The current limit of 120% is a lower threshold for ELsss.

114358.explanation: The municipality should have more influence on where the provincially agreed parking spaces are located. Do not automatically make the first available parking spaces charging spots anymore.

114359.explanation: Except for primary, through traffic roads.

114360.explanation: Always is going too far. That is practically impossible to arrange. Care needs must be leading with respect for everyone's faith, culture, or beliefs.

114362.explanation: Property tax (OZB) on homes should remain at a low level. OZB on non-residential properties should be at an average level.

114368.explanation: With a quantity restriction only for our own residents.

114369.explanation: Solar panels should be placed on roofs.

114374.explanation: It must be appropriate for the particular location.

114377.explanation: With respect for the environment and history of the center. Preferably outside the historic core.

114379.explanation: The needs and capacity of the community are the starting point.

114324.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women.

114325.explanation: A referendum can be a nice and fair way to involve people in decision-making.

114331.explanation: The distribution of asylum seekers according to the distribution law is a good decision. Gennep should take its share in this.

114334.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to promote the heating guide every year. We want to discourage the growth of the number of wood stoves.

114336.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one you share with everyone. A fireworks show organized by the Municipality of Gennep can contribute to this.

114343.explanation: As a child, you are under enormous pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons to be accessible not only to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

114346.explanation: Contributes to the feeling of safety on the streets.

114347.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our view. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be infringed upon lightly. Therefore, we prefer more officials on the street who can immediately intervene if something goes wrong.

114348.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to.

114352.explanation: Permanent habitation of holiday parks is not a sustainable solution for the housing shortage. These homes are not suitable for that purpose. However, a park can temporarily shelter, for example, status holders and refugees from places like Ukraine.

114353.explanation: We mainly build in existing neighborhoods and villages, within the built-up area. We spare the green countryside and green zones within the built-up area as much as possible. New construction is realized on the sites of existing objects or, for example, on outdated industrial sites.

114355.explanation: We do not want people with the lowest incomes in our municipality to get into trouble. We believe that everyone should be able to keep their head above water. That is why we support debt forgiveness.

114357.explanation: Young people are under pressure from all sides, from unaffordable housing to addictive social media algorithms. With youth work, we prevent young people from (further) getting into trouble. We also contribute to safe and stronger neighborhoods where it is nice to live and grow up.

114358.explanation: This stimulates the use of sustainable energy.

114359.explanation: With a lower maximum speed, traffic in our municipality becomes much safer.

114361.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don’t solve this by cutting more and always choosing the cheapest options. We must especially ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

114367.explanation: Status holders are only one of the groups that receive priority. Given the number of applications from status holders in Gennep, this is not a problem.

114368.explanation: This contributes to cleaner streets and a healthy environment.

114369.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by allowing flowers and other low vegetation to grow.

114372.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gennep supports a regulated soft drugs policy.

114379.explanation: This depends on future developments such as membership numbers, need for new sports parks, budget, etc.

114380.explanation: Only if the budget allows it.

114325.explanation: It is important that residents are allowed to contribute their thoughts not only once every four years.

114331.explanation: We believe that people fleeing war, violence, and persecution must always be accommodated. An asylum seekers' center (AZC) is a necessary part of this. The location of an AZC must be determined in consultation with the local population. Only then is there support.

114334.explanation: Open fireplaces and wood stoves cause unnecessarily high emissions of fine particles and harmful combustion gases. This is bad for the environment and causes health problems.

114335.explanation: Terrace heaters are equivalent to energy waste.

114336.explanation: Fireworks are harmful to people and animals and release harmful substances. A central fireworks show is always less harmful than individual fireworks. But a light show with music is even less harmful and just as festive. Moreover, there are insufficient experts in the Netherlands.

114343.explanation: Music education is an important pillar in the cultural and personal development of a child. Every child must have unrestricted access to it. Good music education should also have a place in primary schools.

114346.explanation: Enforcement in the area of safety is primarily the task of the police and judiciary. If budget cuts in this field lead to insufficient capacity, then community enforcement officers (boa's), with their limited powers and resources, are not a solution.

114347.explanation: In some cases and specific locations, camera surveillance can be an effective tool in enforcement policy. Randomly placing cameras everywhere is undesirable.

114348.explanation: There must be sufficient and safe shelter everywhere for women fleeing violent living situations.

114352.explanation: Holiday homes are generally not designed for permanent residence and often do not comply with building regulations. Simply more houses need to be built, and obstacles such as nitrogen regulations must be removed.

114353.explanation: We are against building in nature reserves and forests. No tree felling for housing. Building should be possible on unused or vacant open plots without significant natural value.

114355.explanation: People with low incomes cannot afford municipal taxes. Abolishing remission schemes only leads to more debt problems.

114356.explanation: We want 120% of the social assistance norm as the standard for municipal support.

114357.explanation: Investing in young people is important for the future. More facilities must be created, and an active youth policy pursued. Existing facilities must be better utilized. Currently, there are far too few opportunities for young people.

114358.explanation: If we want to promote more electric driving, we must also ensure the necessary facilities.

114359.explanation: Safety around schools is currently insufficient. The maximum speed limit should be lowered throughout the built-up area.

114360.explanation: Given the limitations we currently face in healthcare, this is an unattainable goal. Everyone has the right to equal treatment in healthcare. From an integration perspective, this is also undesirable.

114361.explanation: The quality of healthcare and quality assurance must always come first.

114362.explanation: Property tax should be more differentiated according to the value of the home. Those with the greatest ability to pay should bear the heaviest burden.

114363.explanation: The dog tax is an unfair levy. Municipal charges should tax citizens proportionally, not just target a single group.

114367.explanation: Refugees with residence status should be able to obtain suitable housing, just like all other urgent housing seekers in the Netherlands. There is a shortage of social rental homes. This shortage must be resolved by building more homes, not by making vulnerable groups compete with each other.

114368.explanation: The amount of waste in the Netherlands must be reduced. Litter must also be combated. It should be made easier for people to dispose of their waste responsibly. Asking for a contribution based on ability to pay is then possible.

114369.explanation: Solar panels belong on the roofs of houses, commercial buildings, and government buildings. Scarce and high-quality land must not be used for this purpose. Vacant agricultural land should be designated for nature or housing construction.

114372.explanation: A drug policy concerning young people must be developed. Ostrich politics are not the answer to threats. The trade in cannabis products is not absent in Gennep. It takes place on the streets and in homes. Mixing with the trade in other substances is a real risk.

114374.explanation: High residential towers do not really fit the rural character of Gennep. However, the need for housing remains large for the time being. High towers should be possible at some locations. We must choose between preserving greenery and livability or building upward.

114375.explanation: Room rental for young people should be possible, and suitable living spaces can be included in municipal building plans for this. Existing living spaces should not be withdrawn from the housing stock for profiteering by unscrupulous landlords.

114377.explanation: Traffic and parking pressure in the center is already very high. Facilitating even more car movements is undesirable. It is better to invest in a sustainable municipal public transport network and to discourage parking in the center.

114379.explanation: Sports practice should be accessible in a low-threshold way. Sports parks in villages are an option. But concentrating sports facilities, such as in the new sports boulevard, can provide a cost-efficient quality boost.

114380.explanation: The Ladies Tour is a nice event for cycling enthusiasts. However, it is also an expensive one-day event with little demonstrable benefit for the municipality of Gennep. It is better to invest the money in sports opportunities for the residents.

statements.title: The municipality should preserve the existing sports parks in the villages.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves in Gennep should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Gennep moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Gennep should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
Gennep should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Gennep should have a women's shelter (refuge for victims of domestic violence).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Tall residential apartment blocks may be allowed in Gennep.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the center of Gennep.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The residents of Gennep should be able to give their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Gennep moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Gennep should remain the start and finish location for the Ladies Tour, the professional women's cycling race.
